name: cluster-F-tdd-rate-limiter
goal: Use test-driven development to implement rate_limiter.py exposing a TokenBucket class with constructor TokenBucket(capacity: int, refill_rate: float) where refill_rate is tokens per second. Methods: try_acquire(tokens: int = 1) -> bool (returns True and consumes tokens if available, else returns False without consuming), available() -> float (returns current token count, may be fractional). Use TDD: write each test first in test_rate_limiter.py before implementing the corresponding behavior. Required tests at minimum: starts at full capacity, try_acquire(1) reduces available by 1, try_acquire returns False when empty, available() refills over time (use time.monotonic-based clock injection or freezegun-equivalent), capacity is the upper bound for refill, multi-token acquire is atomic (no partial). Run pytest and confirm all tests pass.

notes.purpose: Tier 2 TDD-target case. Goal explicitly says 'use test-driven development'. The TDD planning gate (#8) inspects the plan for test→impl pairs and fails any non-exempt impl step that lacks a preceding test step. The clock injection requirement is the non-trivial bit that may need rewriting.
